prompt: Show me the sales in Nov 2021
visualization_goal: Sales trend for Nov 2021, with date as x and sales value as y
gt_sql: SELECT Sold_Date, SUM(Total_Sale_Value) AS Total_Sales_Value FROM sales WHERE CAST(Sold_Date AS VARCHAR) LIKE '2021-11%' GROUP BY Sold_Date ORDER BY Sold_Date
gt_analysis: Sales in November 2021 show daily values ranging from $11,030.39 to $52,004.60, with a total of $499,984.43 across 29 days.

y_data = data_df[config['y_axis']]

prompt: Show the 12 months of 2023 with total revenue and total units sold
visualization_goal: Line chart showing monthly revenue trends for 2023
gt_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', Sold_Date) AS month_start, SUM(Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue, SUM(Qty_Sold) AS total_units FROM sales WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM Sold_Date) = 2023 GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', Sold_Date) ORDER BY month_start ASC
gt_analysis: The 12 months of 2023 show total revenue ranging from $353,284.21 (July) to $874,263.67 (December), with total units from 27,932 to 60,941.

visualization_goal: Bar chart showing total revenue per brand for 2023, ordered from highest to lowest
gt_sql: SELECT p.brand, ROUND(SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value), 2) AS total_revenue FROM sales s JOIN products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded WHERE YEAR(CAST(s.Sold_Date AS DATE)) = 2023 GROUP BY p.brand ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

CrunchTime has the lowest revenue at $452,267.33. The total revenue across all brands amounts to $5,416,118.93.

prompt: Compare average monthly revenue between store regions for 2022 and 2023
visualization_goal: Grouped bar chart comparing average monthly revenue per region between 2022 and 2023
gt_sql: SELECT st.region, s.yr AS year, ROUND(AVG(s.monthly_rev), 2) AS avg_monthly_revenue FROM (SELECT Store_Number, YEAR(CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS yr, DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS month, SUM(Total_Sale_Value) AS monthly_rev FROM sales WHERE YEAR(CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) IN (2022, 2023) GROUP BY Store_Number, yr, month) s JOIN stores st ON s.Store_Number = st.Store_Number GROUP BY st.region, s.yr ORDER BY st.region, s.yr
gt_data:
region,year,avg_monthly_revenue
Midwest,2022,10522.09
Midwest,2023,10821.44
Northeast,2022,17703.52
Northeast,2023,14970.11
South,2022,14925.39
South,2023,14578.71
West,2022,12241.59
West,2023,12129.5
gt_analysis: Northeast has the highest average monthly revenue in both years ($17,703.52 in 2022, $14,970.11 in 2023), although it declined year-over-year. Midwest is the lowest but shows growth from $10,522.09 to $10,821.44. South dropped from $14,925.39 to $14,578.71 and West from $12,241.59 to $12,129.50, both showing a slight decline from 2022 to 2023.

gt_chart_config.title: Avg Monthly Revenue by Region: 2022 vs 2023

gt_chart_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

regions = data_df['region'].unique()
x = np.arange(len(regions))
width = 0.35

df_2022 = data_df[data_df['year'] == 2022].set_index('region')
df_2023 = data_df[data_df['year'] == 2023].set_index('region')

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.bar(x - width/2, [df_2022.loc[r, 'avg_monthly_revenue'] for r in regions], width, label='2022', color='steelblue')
ax.bar(x + width/2, [df_2023.loc[r, 'avg_monthly_revenue'] for r in regions], width, label='2023', color='darkorange')

ax.set_xlabel('region')
ax.set_ylabel('avg_monthly_revenue')
ax.set_title(config['title'])
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(regions)
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

prompt: Show monthly revenue split by promo flag for 2023 as a grouped bar chart
visualization_goal: Grouped bar chart showing monthly revenue in 2023 split between promo and non-promo sales
gt_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS month_start, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN On_Promo = 1 THEN Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END), 2) AS promo_revenue, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN On_Promo = 0 THEN Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END), 2) AS non_promo_revenue FROM sales WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(Sold_Date AS DATE)) = 2023 GROUP BY month_start ORDER BY month_start ASC
gt_analysis: Monthly non-promo revenue peaks in December at $835,289.24, with promo revenue highest in December ($38,974.43) and November ($35,099.91). February, March, June, and July had no promo sales.
gt_chart_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

x = np.arange(len(data_df))
y_cols = config['y_columns']
width = 0.8 / len(y_cols)

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
for i, col in enumerate(y_cols):
    offset = (i - (len(y_cols) - 1) / 2) * width
    ax.bar(x + offset, data_df[col], width, label=col.replace('_', ' ').title())

ax.set_xlabel(config['x_axis'])
ax.set_ylabel('Revenue')
ax.set_title(config['title'])
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels([str(v) for v in data_df[config['x_axis']]], rotation=45, ha='right')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

prompt: Return the top 3 brands by realized average selling price in 2023, where average price is computed as total revenue divided by total units sold, along with total units sold per brand
gt_sql: SELECT p.brand, ROUND(SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) / NULLIF(SUM(s.Qty_Sold), 0), 4) AS avg_realized_unit_price, SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS total_units FROM sales s JOIN products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(s.Sold_Date AS DATE)) = 2023 GROUP BY p.brand HAVING SUM(s.Qty_Sold) > 0 ORDER BY avg_realized_unit_price DESC, total_units DESC, p.brand ASC LIMIT 3
gt_analysis: The top 3 brands by avg realized price are CrunchTime (18.2241), GlowUp (17.9275), and CleanHome (17.1089).

gt_chart_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

pivot_df = data_df.pivot(index=config['x_axis'], columns=config['group_by'], values=config['y_axis'])
x = np.arange(len(pivot_df.index))
width = 0.8 / max(len(pivot_df.columns), 1)

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
for i, group in enumerate(pivot_df.columns):
    offset = (i - (len(pivot_df.columns) - 1) / 2) * width
    ax.bar(x + offset, pivot_df[group].values, width, label=str(group))

ax.set_xlabel(config['x_axis'])
ax.set_ylabel(config['y_axis'])
ax.set_title(config['title'])
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels([str(v) for v in pivot_df.index], rotation=45, ha='right')
ax.legend(title=str(config['group_by']))
ax.grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

prompt: Return the top 10 region-category combinations by year-over-year revenue growth from 2022 to 2023, showing revenue for both years and the growth expressed as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places
gt_sql: WITH yearly AS (SELECT st.region, p.category, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(s.Sold_Date AS DATE)) AS yr, SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS revenue FROM sales s JOIN stores st ON s.Store_Number = st.Store_Number JOIN products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(s.Sold_Date AS DATE)) IN (2022, 2023) GROUP BY st.region, p.category, yr) SELECT region, category, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = 2022 THEN revenue ELSE 0 END), 2) AS revenue_2022, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = 2023 THEN revenue ELSE 0 END), 2) AS revenue_2023, ROUND(((SUM(CASE WHEN yr = 2023 THEN revenue ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN yr = 2022 THEN revenue ELSE 0 END)) / NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN yr = 2022 THEN revenue ELSE 0 END), 0)) * 100, 2) AS yoy_growth_pct FROM yearly GROUP BY region, category HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN yr = 2022 THEN revenue ELSE 0 END) > 0 ORDER BY yoy_growth_pct DESC, revenue_2023 DESC, region ASC, category ASC LIMIT 10
gt_analysis: The first row is Midwest with category=Dairy, revenue_2022=109,924.97, revenue_2023=177,962.09, yoy_growth_pct=61.89%.
